title: Save and Load — File I/O
description: Add file I/O to persist student records. Write a saveToFile() function that writes all student data to a text file using ofstream, and a loadFromFile() function that reads records back using ifstream. The user should be able to save their roster and reload it later.
test_code:
assert '#include <fstream>' in cpp, 'Must include <fstream> header for file I/O'
assert 'ofstream' in cpp, 'Must use ofstream to write to a file'
assert 'ifstream' in cpp, 'Must use ifstream to read from a file'
assert 'open' in cpp or 'ofstream' in cpp, 'Must open a file for writing'
assert 'saveToFile' in cpp or 'save_to_file' in cpp or 'saveFile' in cpp, 'Must define a save function'
assert 'loadFromFile' in cpp or 'load_from_file' in cpp or 'loadFile' in cpp, 'Must define a load function'
acceptance_criteria: Defines saveToFile() that writes student data using ofstream
Defines loadFromFile() that reads student data using ifstream
Save and load functions handle the Student struct data
File operations are used in main (e.g., via a menu)
hints: Open for writing: ofstream outFile(filename); outFile << s.name << endl;
Open for reading: ifstream inFile(filename); getline(inFile, s.name);
Write grades on one line: for (int i = 0; i < NUM_GRADES; i++) outFile << s.grades[i] << ' ';
Always close files or let the destructor handle it when the stream goes out of scope
